question: Will Iga Świątek reach the 2026 French Open women's singles final?
textDescription:
The 2026 Roland-Garros women's singles final is scheduled for June 6, 2026 (Saturday). Świątek is a four-time French Open champion with a dominant clay record; Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff, and Mirra Andreeva are top contenders.

Resolution

Resolves YES if Iga Świątek is listed as one of the two finalists in the official 2026 Roland-Garros women's singles draw (regardless of whether she wins).

Resolves NO if she is eliminated before the final, withdraws, or does not enter.

Resolves N/A if the tournament is cancelled or postponed past 2026-07-15.

WTA and rolandgarros.com are the authoritative sources.
newsShortReason: Iga Świątek's strong clay-court performance and recent first-round victory at the 2026 French Open suggest a high likelihood of reaching the final.
newsKeyEvidence: Iga Świątek has won four French Open titles and has a dominant clay-court record. | She advanced to the second round of the 2026 French Open with a 6-1, 6-2 victory over Emerson Jones. | Her next opponent is Sara Bejlek, a player she is favored to defeat. | Top contenders like Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff, and Mirra Andreeva are in the opposite half of the draw, reducing the likelihood of early elimination. | No reports indicate any injuries or issues that might hinder her performance. | The tournament is scheduled to conclude on June 6, 2026, with Świątek aiming for her fifth French Open title.
